questions.prompt:
Refer to the following code fragment:

 double answer = 13 / 5; System.out.println("13 / 5 = " + answer);

The output is:

13 / 5 = 2.0

The programmer intends the output to be:

13 / 5 = 2.6

Which of the following replacements for the first line of code will not fix the problem?
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
/** @param n a 4-digit integer

 * @return true if n is valid, false otherwise */ boolean checkNumber(int n) { int d1,d2,d3,checkDigit,nRemaining,rem; //strip off digits checkDigit = n % 10; nRemaining = n / 10; d3 = nRemaining % 10; nRemaining /= 10; d2 = nRemaining % 10; nRemaining /= 10; d1 = nRemaining % 10; //check validity rem = (d1 + d2 + d3) % 7; return rem == checkDigit; }

A program invokes method checkNumber with the statement:

boolean valid = checkNumber(num);

Which of the following values of num will result in valid having a value of true?
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Given that a, b, and c are integers, consider the boolean expression:

 (a < b) || !( (c == a * b) && (c < a) )

Which of the following will guarantee that the expression is true?

options.content:
E.

c == a * b is true, and c < a is true.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following code segment:

 int x = 7; int y = 3; if ((x < 10) && (y < 0)) System.out.println("Value is: " + x * y); else System.out.println("Value is: " + x / y);

What is printed as a result of executing the code segment?

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method:

 /** Precondition: y > 0 * x * y < Integer.MAX_VALUE */ public int doCalculation(int x, int y) { int result = 0; if (x > 0) { if (x % y == 0) { result = x * y; } } else { result = Math.abs(x); } return result; }

Which of the following best describes the return value when the method is executed?

options.content:
A.

The method returns the product of x and y when x is positive and greater than y;
otherwise it returns the absolute value of x.

B.

The method returns the product of x and y when x is positive and greater than y;
it returns the absolute value of x when x is negative, otherwise it returns 0.

C.

The method returns the product ofx and y when x is positive and a multiple of y, otherwise it returns 0.

D.

The method returns the product of x and y when x is positive and a multiple of y;
it returns the absolute value of x when x is negative,v otherwise it returns 0.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following methods:

 public boolean checker1(int x, double y) { return ((x / 3) == (y / 3)); } public boolean checker2(double x, int y) { return ((x / 3) == (y / 3)); }

Consider the following expression, which appears in a method in the same class as checker1 and checker2:

 checker1(5, 5.0) == checker2(5.0, 5)

Which of the following best describes the result when the expression is evaluated?

options.content:
A.

The expression evaluates to true because both method calls return true.

B.

The expression evaluates to true because both method calls return false.

C.

The expression evaluates to false because the checker1 method call returns true and the checker2 method call returns false.

D.

The expression evaluates to false because the checker1 method call returns false and the checker2 method call returns true.

E.

The expression cannot be evaluated because a runtime error occurs.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method:

/** Precondition: x > 0, y > 0 */ public boolean checkStuff(int x, int y) { return ((x % 10) == y); }

Which of the following best describes the behavior of the method?

options.content:
A.

It returns true when x is 10 times y and false otherwise.

B.

It returns true when x is 10 more than y and false otherwise.

C.

It returns true when x is 10 more than a multiple of y and false otherwise.

D.

It returns true when y is equal to the leftmost digit of x and false otherwise.

E.

It returns true when y is equal to the rightmost digit of x and false otherwise.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Given that n and count are both of type int, which statement is true about the following code segments?

I:

 for (count = 1; count <= n; count++) System.out.println(count);

II:

 count = 1; while (count <= n) { System.out.println(count); count++; }

options.content:
A.

I and II are exactly equivalent for all input values n.

B.

I and II are exactly equivalent for all input values n ≥ 1, but differ when n ≤ 0.

C.

I and II are exactly equivalent only when n = 0.

D.

I and II are exactly equivalent only when n is even.

E.

I and II are not equivalent for any input values of n.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Which of the following program fragments will produce this output? (Ignore spacing.)

 2 - - - - - - 4 - - - - - - 6 - - - - - - 8 - - - - - - 10 - - - - - - 12

I

 for (int i = 1; i <= 6; i++) { for (int k = 1; k <= 6; k++) if (k == i) System.out.print(2 * k); else System.out.print("-"); System.out.println(); }

II

 for (int i = 1; i <= 6; i++) { for (int k = 1; k <= i - 1; k++) System.out.print("-"); System.out.print(2 * i); for (int k = 1; k <= 6 - i; k++) System.out.print("-"); System.out.println(); }

III

 for (int i = 1; i <= 6; i++) { for (int k = 1; k <= i - 1; k++) System.out.print("-"); System.out.print(2 * i); for (int k = i + 1; k <= 6; k++) System.out.print("-"); System.out.println(); }
questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

II.

questions.prompt:
Which of the following three code segments will produce the same output?

I.

 int i = 1; while (i < 12) { System.out.print(i + " "); i *= 2; }

II.

 for (int i = 4; i > 0; i--) { System.out.print((4 - i) * 2 + 1 + " "); }

III.

 for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) { System.out.print((int)Math.pow(2, i) + " "); }

  • II. x != 6

Which of the following expressions can replace /* missing condition */ so that the code segment works as intended?

  • II. val <= 40

questions.prompt:
Refer to the following code segment. You may assume that arr is an array of int values.

 int sum = arr[0], i = 0; while (i < arr.length) { i++; sum += arr[i]; }

Which of the following will be the result of executing the segment?

options.content:
A.

Sum of arr[0], arr[1], ..., arr[arr.length-1] will be stored in sum.

B.

Sum of arr[1], arr[2], ..., arr[arr.length-1] will be stored in sum.

C.

Sum of arr[0], arr[1], ..., arr[arr.length] will be stored in sum.

 int[] arr = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7}; for (int k = 3; k < arr.length - 1; k++) arr[k] = arr[k + 1];

Which of the following represents the contents of arr as a result of executing the code segment?

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method:

 public void mystery(int[] data) { for (int k = 0; k < data.length - 1; k++) data[k + 1] = data[k] + data[k + 1]; }

The following code segment appears in another method in the same class:

 int[] values = {5, 2, 1, 3, 8}; mystery(values); for (int v : values) System.out.print(v + " "); System.out.println();

What is printed as a result of executing the code segment?

options.content:
E.

Nothing is printed because an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is thrown during the execution of method mystery.

questions.prompt:
Consider the following method, which is intended to return the maximum integer value contained in ranges.

 public int findMaxValue(int[] ranges) { int max = 0; for (int value : ranges) { if (value > max) { max = value; } } return max; }

The method does not always work as intended. Which of the following initializations for ranges can be used to show that findMaxValue does not work as intended?

options.content:
A.

An array containing consecutive integers from -75 to -25, inclusive

B.

An array containing consecutive integers from -50 to 0, inclusive

C.

An array containing consecutive integers from -25 to 25, inclusive

D.

An array containing consecutive integers from 0 to 50, inclusive

E.

An array containing consecutive integers from 25 to 75, inclusive

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt:
Which of the following, when used as the /* body */ of method sum, will enable that method to compute 1 + 2 + ... + n correctly for any n > 0?

 /** @param n a positive integer * @return 1 + 2 + ... + n */ public int sum(int n) { /* body */ } 
 I return n + sum(n - 1); 
 II if (n == 1) return 1; else return n + sum(n - 1); 
 III if (n == 1) return 1; else return sum(n) + sum(n - 1);

questions.prompt:
Consider the following class:

 /** A class that sorts an array of Integer objects from * largest to smallest using a selection sort. */ public class Sorter { private Integer[] a; public Sorter(Integer[] arr) { a = arr; } /** Swap a[i] and a[j] in array a. */ private void swap(int i, int j) { /* implementation not shown */ } /** Sort array a from largest to smallest using selection sort. * Precondition: a is an array of Integer objects. */ public void selectionSort() { for (int i = 0; i < a.length - 1; i++) { // find max element in a[i+1] to a[n-1] Integer max = a[i]; int maxPos = i; for (int j = i + 1; j < a.length; j++) if (max.compareTo(a[j]) < 0) // max less than a[j] { max = a[j]; maxPos = j; } swap(i, maxPos); // swap a[i] and a[maxPos] } } }

If an array of Integer contains the following elements, what would the array look like after the third pass of selectionSort, sorting from high to low?

 89 42 -3 13 109 70 2
